title: mentha arvensis f. procumbens
intro: Mentha arvensis f. procumbens, also known as procumbent field mint, is a subspecies of the field mint plant (Mentha arvensis) that is a low-growing, spreading perennial native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating digestive issues, relieving headaches and colds, and reducing fever. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis f. procumbens include its ability to improve digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
